assertions.title: SAE model loads successfully

steps.expected: SAE weights load into memory on CPU without OSError or RuntimeError
steps.observed: TestSAELoading::test_sae_loads_without_error PASSED — SAE loaded from gemma-scope-2b-pt-res-canonical layer_20/width_16k/canonical

steps.expected: SAE has d_in and d_sae attributes with positive values; d_in matches Gemma 2 2B d_model (2304)
SAE has W_enc with correct shape (d_in, d_sae)
steps.observed: TestSAELoading::test_sae_has_encoder_weights PASSED — W_enc shape matches d_in x d_sae

steps.expected: Base model (~1B param) loads on CPU (requires HF_TOKEN)
steps.observed: All 4 TestBaseModelLoading tests SKIPPED — HF_TOKEN not set (expected behavior, tests are skip-marked for gated model access)

assertions.note: SAE loading fully validated. Base model loading is correctly SKIP-marked and requires HF_TOKEN for gated Gemma 2 2B access. This is expected and per design.

steps.action: Create random input tensor of shape (batch=2, seq_len=32, d_model=2304)
Run SAE inference and check output shape
Test with mock activation where monitored index 0 (0.1) is below threshold 0.5
Test with mock activation where monitored index 3 (0.9) exceeds threshold 0.5
Test with multiple monitored indices where at least one exceeds threshold
steps.observed: PASSED — returns True when monitoring indices (0, 3) with 0.9 > 0.5

steps.action: Test any-index-exceeds logic across batch and sequence dimensions
steps.expected: Returns True if ANY token in ANY batch item exceeds threshold at monitored index

evidence.testResults: All 4 relevant tests PASSED: test_exceeds_threshold_returns_true, test_below_threshold_returns_false, test_exact_threshold_is_not_exceeded, test_any_index_exceeds_returns_true

assertions.title: Tensor shape invariance under batch size

steps.action: Test batch_size=1: activations shape (1, 16, d_model) -> feature_acts.shape[-1] == n_sae_features
Test batch_size=4: activations shape (4, 16, d_model) -> feature_acts.shape[-1] == n_sae_features
Test batch_size=8: activations shape (8, 16, d_model) -> feature_acts.shape[-1] == n_sae_features
Test batch_size=16: activations shape (16, 16, d_model) -> feature_acts.shape[-1] == n_sae_features

evidence.testResults: TestSAEInference::test_activation_shape_invariant_batch_size PASSED — all 4 batch sizes (1, 4, 8, 16) produce same feature dimension

frictions.description: Two DeprecationWarnings from sae-lens SwigPyPacked/SwigPyObject builtin types during SAE loading — cosmetic only, does not affect functionality
frictions.resolution: No action needed; warnings are from third-party sae-lens dependency's use of Swig bindings

summary: Tested 5 assertions (VAL-DATA-001 through VAL-DATA-004, VAL-DATA-008): 5 passed. VAL-DATA-001 is partially testable — SAE loading confirmed working (3 tests passed), base model loading correctly SKIP-marked due to HF_TOKEN requirement for gated Gemma 2 2B access. All threshold logic, feature extraction shapes, and batch invariance tests pass cleanly. 28 of 34 tests passed, 6 skipped (expected), 0 failed. Execution time: ~9.3 seconds on CPU.
